regimeSub: Equities melt up on US-Iran de-escalation while a hawkish Warsh Fed sits on 4.2% CPI with no cutting bias.
themes: US-Iran truce caps Brent near $73 -> oil shock unwinds, but energy already pushed CPI to 3-yr high of 4.2%.
New Fed chair Warsh strips cutting bias -> September HIKE risk live as inflation reaccelerates.
AI/semis ($7trn buildout) drive SPX 7440 and Dow 52k -> narrow tech leadership masks valuation risk.
Trump-Fed clash (Cook firing blocked by SCOTUS) -> independence premium and term-premium risk building in long end.

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = negative real rate; Fed restrictive in name only, fuels risk assets.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +31bp (4.07/4.38), modestly steep; hike risk flattens front, fiscal/term premium pins long end.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight; VIX 17.6 and equity highs signal no credit stress, complacency into H2.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 17.65, calm post-Iran deal; cheap hedges given Fed-hike and Fed-independence tail risks.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $73 after US-Iran halt; geopolitical premium drained, but already embedded in 4.2% CPI.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4003 near record; hedging sticky inflation, negative real rates and Fed-independence erosion.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1546 weak KRW despite KOSPI 8643 melt-up; AI/chip equity inflows not lifting won, BoK divergence.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Nvidia/Micron/Broadcom carry the tape; AI energy trade is the market's single point of failure.

headline: Risk assets are partying at record highs on a US-Iran ceasefire and an AI capex boom, even as a hawkish Warsh Fed stares down 4.2% inflation and a live September hike.
